01 // ABOUT

About Me

U1 — CGHC
EE-STUDENT-2029 // UTEP

I'm an Electrical Engineering student at the University of Texas at El Paso with a 4.0 GPA, a technical background shaped by hands-on repair work since age 14, and a proven record of winning in engineering, pitch, and case competitions at both regional and international levels.

My focus is hardware engineering, embedded systems,and entrepreneurship. I build systems end to end, from early concept to functional prototype, including a Braille accessibility device that won $5,000 across three pitch competitions, a telemetry ground station for UTEP’s rocket team, and a 4-bit calculator built entirely from discrete transistors.

Beyond engineering, I bring discipline, curiosity, and competitive drive to everything I do. And consistently put myself in rooms where I can learn fast, build fast, and perform under pressure.

02 // PROJECTS

Featured Projects

Product render — six solenoid pins generate tactile Braille characters
Smartphone → Bluetooth → Sense Cube → Tactile Braille output
1st Place · $4,000 Total Prize · 3 Competition Wins

The Sense Cube

Wireless handheld device translating real-time smartphone messages into tactile Braille via six high-precision solenoid pins. Designed for blind and visually impaired users. Won across three competitions — Miner Pitch (1st, $3K), Pioneers 21 (1st, $2K), Miner Tank (2nd).

ArduinoBluetoothSolenoids3D PrintingAccessibilityEmbedded Systems
Active

Telemetry Ground Station

Real-time telemetry for flight data — Sun City Rocket Team.

Details

Embedded DAQ interfacing pressure sensors and K-type thermocouples. Team: 2nd IREC 2025, 1st 2025 Lone Star Cup.

05 // AWARDS

Awards & Recognition

1st Place — $3,000 Award

Miners Pitch Competition

Mike Loya Center · UTEP · Nov 2025

Selected from over 60 applications to pitch The Sense Cube. Translates smartphone messages into real-time Braille using six precision solenoid pins. Took 1st place.

2nd Place — Bilingual Pitch

Miner Tank Pitch Competition

Texas Western Hall · UTEP · 2025

El Paso's first bilingual pitch. Answered a judge's question entirely in Spanish for fuller expression of the product's social impact.

1st Place — $1,000 Award

Pioneers 21 — Tacos & Tech-ila

Holiday Mixer · Microsoft & Nusenda · Dec 2025

Won against ventures including Wealthy, NG Leg Bot, Signology LLC, and BrightSteps across the El Paso–Las Cruces corridor.

Dell Tech Academy

Supply Chain · Dell Technologies · 2025

Two-day case competition. Collaborated with Isaac Torres, Aden Ramirez, and Luis Cruz Garfio on optimization strategies.
